title: IM A BOY, IM 35!
description: A sports-culture reflection on Mike Gundy, Michael Chandler, identity, and how a quoted cadence becomes folklore beyond its original context.

motifMap.meaning: The hair becomes an outward totem of Oklahoma State weirdness, stubbornness, and performed identity.
The phrase compresses a 2007 press-conference moment into a reusable cadence in American sports memory.
The post explicitly frames cadence as carrying person, context, masculinity, absurdity, sincerity, and internet memory at once.

excerpt: The cadence itself has become the reference. You don’t need the exact words, and you don’t need the context.

editHistory.notes: Established live extractor failed because shell DNS could not resolve wanderersburn.substack.com; source text was acquired through a live in-app browser read of the supplied URL, then cached as local source packets for validation.

Classified independently as Totem Current / Historical Compression / Adaptive Witness because the post studies a sports phrase and cadence becoming a reusable cultural totem detached from its original context.
